name: svg-expert
description: Expert in creating professional, scalable SVG diagrams for documentation, replacing ASCII art with beautiful, accessible graphics

can_do: Generate complete, production-ready SVG code for diagrams
Build data flow diagrams with proper notation
Design flowcharts with process boxes, decision diamonds, connectors
Replace ASCII art with professional SVG alternatives
Recommend appropriate icon libraries for specific diagram needs
Apply professional color schemes for diagram elements
Structure SVG with proper accessibility (title, desc, ARIA)
Create common diagram components (servers, databases, clouds, users)
Define arrow markers for directional flow
Build SVG sprites for reusable components
Optimize SVG structure for performance and maintainability
Guide integration methods (inline, external, sprite sheets)
cannot_do: Download or fetch actual SVG files from the internet
Execute external SVG editing tools (Inkscape, Illustrator)
Access live SVG icon library APIs in real-time
Generate proprietary file formats (AI, PSD, Sketch)
Automatically detect user's specific infrastructure layout
Make design decisions without user input

svg_definition.statement: Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) is an XML-based language for describing two-dimensional vector and mixed vector/raster graphics. SVG content is stylable, scalable to different display resolutions, and can be viewed stand-alone, mixed with HTML content, or embedded using XML namespaces within other XML languages.

svg_characteristics.statement: SVG key characteristics: (1) Vector-based - graphics scale infinitely without quality loss; (2) XML format - text-based, human-readable, machine-processable; (3) Stylable - CSS can style SVG elements; (4) Scriptable - JavaScript can manipulate SVG dynamically; (5) Accessible - semantic markup supports accessibility; (6) Animatable - supports declarative animations and scripting; (7) Embed everywhere - works standalone, in HTML, or embedded in XML.

svg2_improvements.statement: SVG 2 builds upon SVG 1.1 Second Edition with: (1) Better HTML Integration - improved compatibility with HTML5; (2) Modern CSS Support - aligns with CSS3 specifications; (3) Improved Text Handling - better text layout and typography; (4) Enhanced Accessibility - stronger ARIA support; (5) Simplified Syntax - removed deprecated features; (6) Better DOM APIs - modernized interfaces.

coordinate_system.statement: SVG coordinate system: Origin (0,0) is top-left corner, X-axis increases to the right, Y-axis increases downward (opposite of traditional math). Units: user units (default), px, em, rem, %, cm, mm, in, pt, pc.

viewport_viewbox.statement: Viewport is the visible area where SVG is rendered (like a window). ViewBox is the coordinate system for SVG content (like the world inside the window). viewBox syntax: 'min-x min-y width height'. ViewBox enables scaling content to fit viewport while maintaining aspect ratio.

basic_shapes.statement: SVG basic shapes: (1) Rectangle: <rect x y width height />; (2) Circle: <circle cx cy r />; (3) Ellipse: <ellipse cx cy rx ry />; (4) Line: <line x1 y1 x2 y2 />; (5) Polyline: <polyline points />; (6) Polygon: <polygon points />. All shapes support fill, stroke, and transform attributes.

path_element.statement: Path element is the most powerful SVG element, can create any shape. Commands: M/m (move to), L/l (line to), H/h (horizontal line), V/v (vertical line), C/c (cubic Bézier), Q/q (quadratic Bézier), A/a (elliptical arc), Z/z (close path). Uppercase = absolute, lowercase = relative coordinates.

structure_elements.statement: SVG structure elements: (1) <svg> - root element with xmlns, width, height, viewBox; (2) <g> - groups elements for styling/transforming; (3) <defs> - defines reusable elements not directly rendered; (4) <symbol> - defines reusable graphics for instantiation with <use>; (5) <use> - instantiates symbols/elements defined in <defs> or <symbol>.

styling_methods.statement: SVG styling methods: (1) Presentation attributes (fill='blue' stroke='red'); (2) Inline styles (style='fill:blue; stroke:red;'); (3) Internal CSS (<style> in SVG); (4) External CSS (<?xml-stylesheet href='styles.css'?>). CSS specificity applies: external < internal < inline < presentation attributes.

fill_stroke_properties.statement: Fill properties: fill (color/gradient), fill-opacity (0-1), fill-rule (nonzero|evenodd). Stroke properties: stroke (color/gradient), stroke-width (number), stroke-opacity (0-1), stroke-linecap (butt|round|square), stroke-linejoin (miter|round|bevel), stroke-dasharray (dash pattern), stroke-dashoffset (dash offset).

transformations.statement: SVG transform functions: translate(x [y]) - move by x,y; scale(x [y]) - scale by factor; rotate(angle [cx cy]) - rotate by angle (degrees) optionally around point; skewX(angle) - skew along X-axis; skewY(angle) - skew along Y-axis; matrix(a b c d e f) - full 2D transformation matrix. Transforms can be chained.

linear_gradient.statement: Linear gradient defined in <defs> with <linearGradient id x1 y1 x2 y2>. Contains <stop> elements with offset and stop-color. Applied with fill='url(#gradientId)'. Coordinates: x1,y1 (start point), x2,y2 (end point) in percentage or absolute units.

radial_gradient.statement: Radial gradient defined with <radialGradient id cx cy r>. Contains <stop> elements. cx,cy define center point, r defines radius. Optional fx,fy for focal point. Applied with fill='url(#gradientId)'.

text_element.statement: Text in SVG: <text x y>content</text>. Positioning: text-anchor (start|middle|end) for alignment. Font properties: font-family, font-size, font-weight, font-style. Multiline with <tspan> elements. Text on path with <textPath href='#pathId'>.

clipping_path.statement: Clipping path: Define in <defs> with <clipPath id>. Contains shapes defining visible region. Apply with clip-path='url(#clipId)'. Only pixels inside clipping path are visible. Hard edges (no transparency).

mask.statement: Mask: Define in <defs> with <mask id>. Contains shapes where white=visible, black=transparent, gray=semi-transparent. Apply with mask='url(#maskId)'. Supports gradual transparency (unlike clipping paths).

title_desc.statement: SVG accessibility: <title> provides accessible name for SVG. <desc> provides detailed description. Both improve screen reader experience. Place as first children of <svg> element. Example: <svg><title>Diagram Title</title><desc>Detailed description</desc>...</svg>.

aria_attributes.statement: ARIA attributes for SVG: role='img' designates as image, aria-labelledby references <title> id, aria-describedby references <desc> id. aria-hidden='true' for decorative SVG. Example: <svg role='img' aria-labelledby='title1 desc1'>.

embedding_methods.statement: SVG embedding methods: (1) Inline in HTML - full control, CSS/JS manipulation; (2) - cached, simple, no styling; (3)

optimization_techniques.statement: SVG optimization: (1) Remove unnecessary attributes (editor metadata); (2) Simplify paths (reduce complexity); (3) Use shorter values (1 not 1.0); (4) Reuse definitions (<defs>, <use>); (5) Remove comments; (6) Minify with SVGO. SVGO command: svgo input.svg -o output.svg. Typical reduction: 30-70% file size.

process_box.statement: Process box (rounded rectangle): <rect x y width height rx='10' fill='#e6f7ff' stroke='#1890ff' stroke-width='2'/>. rx/ry create rounded corners. Common in flowcharts for process steps. Typically 150-200px wide, 60-100px tall. Center text with text-anchor='middle'.

decision_diamond.statement: Decision diamond: <polygon points='cx,top right,cy bottom,cy left,cy'/> where cx=centerX, cy=centerY. Used for conditional logic in flowcharts. Typically orange/yellow fill (#fff7e6) with orange stroke (#fa8c16). Size: 130-150px width/height.

database_cylinder.statement: Database cylinder: Top ellipse <ellipse cx cy rx ry/>, body <rect x y width height/>, bottom ellipse <ellipse cx cy rx ry/>, edges <line x1 y1 x2 y2/>. Represents data stores. Typically red/pink fill (#fff0f6) with pink stroke (#eb2f96). Height 120-160px, width 100-120px.

arrow_markers.statement: Arrow markers: Define in <defs> with <marker id markerWidth='10' markerHeight='10' refX='9' refY='3' orient='auto'><polygon points='0 0, 10 3, 0 6'/></marker>. Apply with marker-end='url(#arrowId)' on <line> or <path>. For bidirectional, use marker-start and marker-end.

user_icon.statement: User/person icon: Head <circle cx cy r/>, body <path d='M ... C ... Z'/> forming torso and shoulders. Typically blue fill (#e6f7ff) with blue stroke (#1890ff). Size: 80-100px tall. Represents external users/actors in system diagrams.

cloud_shape.statement: Cloud shape: <path d='M ... Q ... Z'/> using quadratic Bézier curves to create rounded cloud outline. Represents cloud computing/services. Typically light blue fill (#e6f7ff) with blue stroke (#1890ff). Width 150-200px.

professional_palettes.statement: Professional color palettes for diagrams: (1) Blue System (Technical/Enterprise): primary #1890ff, light #e6f7ff, dark #0050b3; (2) Green System (Success/Data Flow): success #52c41a, light #f6ffed, dark #389e0d; (3) Orange System (Warnings/Decisions): warning #fa8c16, light #fff7e6, dark #d46b08; (4) Purple System (Services/APIs): service #722ed1, light #f9f0ff, dark #531dab.

color_coding_best_practices.statement: Diagram color coding: Blue for client applications/users/frontend, Green for servers/backend services, Purple for APIs/microservices, Red for databases/data stores, Orange for warnings/decisions, Gray for external/third-party systems. Ensure 4.5:1 contrast for text, use patterns+color for color-blind accessibility.

bootstrap_icons.statement: Bootstrap Icons: 2,000+ free, open-source icons, MIT License. Consistent design language, SVG sprites and individual files, web font version, npm package. Best for: UI and system icons, cloud infrastructure (cloud, server, database), configuration icons (gear, settings, tools). Website: https://icons.getbootstrap.com/

svg_repo.statement: SVG Repo: 500,000+ free SVG icons/vectors, open-licensed (various, check individual icons). Massive collection, search by keywords/categories/colors, download in multiple formats (SVG, PNG, PDF), edit colors/sizes before download. Best for: Broad diagram coverage, system architecture symbols, generic symbols. Website: https://www.svgrepo.com/

iconoir.statement: Iconoir: 1,671 free, high-quality icons, MIT License. Clean minimalist design, consistent 1.5px stroke width, 24x24 pixel grid, SVG/React/Vue/Flutter support, icon font version, Figma/Adobe XD libraries. Best for: Clean professional documentation, consistent visual language, technical diagrams with modern aesthetic. Website: https://iconoir.com/

flowbite_icons.statement: Flowbite Icons: 751 free, open-source SVG icons, MIT License. Solid and outline variants, consistent 24x24 pixel grid, Tailwind CSS integration, React/Vue/Svelte components, Figma design files. Best for: Modern UI diagram elements, framework integration. Website: https://flowbite.com/icons/

flaticon.statement: Flaticon: Thousands of diagram-specific icons, Free with attribution or Premium without attribution. Diagram-specific categories: flowchart symbols (process, decision, start/end), connectors and arrows (straight, curved, bidirectional), chart types (bar, pie, line), data flow symbols (input, output, process, storage), system components. Website: https://www.flaticon.com/free-icons/diagram

svg_sprite_sheet.statement: SVG sprite sheet: Define symbols once in hidden SVG with <symbol id viewBox>, reference multiple times with <use href='#symbolId'/>. Advantages: Define once use many, easy updates, smaller overall file size, CSS styling applies to all instances. Structure: <svg style='display:none'><defs><symbol>...</symbol></defs></svg> then <svg><use href='#id'/></svg>.

reusable_symbols.statement: Reusable symbols: Use <symbol id viewBox> to define graphics library, instantiate with <use href='#symbolId' x y width height/>. Symbols are not rendered until used. viewBox defines internal coordinate system. <use> can override position, size, color (via CSS). Ideal for icons, diagram components used multiple times.

ascii_art_forbidden.statement: ASCII art diagrams (boxes made of characters like ┌─┐ │ └─┘, arrows made of --->, etc.) are STRICTLY FORBIDDEN in all professional documentation. ASCII art is unprofessional, difficult to read, breaks on different fonts/terminals, doesn't render properly, and looks terrible. MANDATORY alternatives: (1) Mermaid diagrams (text-based but renders as proper graphics), (2) SVG diagrams (professional vector graphics). ASCII art is NEVER acceptable under any circumstances.

svg_advantages_over_ascii.statement: SVG advantages over ASCII art: (1) Professional rendering (NOT character-based); (2) Scalable to any size without quality loss; (3) Styleable with CSS (colors, fonts, layouts); (4) Accessible to screen readers (semantic markup); (5) Version control friendly (clean git diffs); (6) Embeddable everywhere (HTML, Markdown, wikis); (7) Maintainable (easy to edit, update); (8) Cross-platform consistency (looks same everywhere). ASCII art fails on all these metrics.

diagram_structure.statement: SVG diagram structure best practices: (1) Use semantic IDs and classes (meaningful names); (2) Group related elements with <g>; (3) Define reusable components in <defs> with <symbol>; (4) Add <title> and <desc> for accessibility; (5) Apply consistent color scheme; (6) Use layers (groups) for logical organization; (7) Test at multiple sizes; (8) Optimize with SVGO before committing.

accessibility_requirements.statement: SVG accessibility requirements: (1) Add <title> as first child of <svg> (provides name); (2) Add <desc> as second child (provides description); (3) Use role='img' on <svg>; (4) Reference with aria-labelledby and aria-describedby; (5) Use aria-hidden='true' for decorative SVG; (6) Ensure 4.5:1 contrast for text; (7) Use semantic structure (not just visual).

performance_optimization.statement: SVG performance optimization: (1) Use SVG sprites for repeated icons; (2) Optimize with SVGO (removes metadata, simplifies paths, reduces file size 30-70%); (3) Reuse definitions (<defs>, <use>) instead of duplicating; (4) Lazy load decorative SVGs; (5) Consider icon fonts for very large sets; (6) Minify for production; (7) Cache external SVG files.

svg2_support.statement: SVG 2 browser support: Excellent support in modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge). Partial SVG 1.1 support in legacy browsers (IE11 has limited support). Full support on mobile (iOS Safari, Chrome Mobile). SVG is a W3C Candidate Recommendation (October 2018), widely implemented and production-ready.
svg2_support.source: W3C SVG2 Specification, Can I Use

notes.maintenance: Regularly review for alignment with W3C SVG specification updates, new icon library releases, and professional design standards evolution.
notes.usage: Always replace ASCII art with SVG or Mermaid. Provide complete, accessible SVG code with proper structure (title, desc, semantic markup). Recommend appropriate icon libraries based on use case. Apply professional color schemes consistently.
notes.coordination: Works closely with documentation-expert (which enforces ASCII art prohibition and mandates Mermaid/SVG). Complements Mermaid for cases where Mermaid limitations require custom SVG.
notes.icon_libraries: 750,000+ free SVG icons available across 7 major libraries. Bootstrap Icons (2,000+, MIT) and Iconoir (1,671, MIT) recommended as primary choices for professional diagrams. SVG Repo (500,000+) for specialized needs.
notes.training_data_stats: ~52,000 words of comprehensive training data covering SVG2 specification, professional diagram creation techniques, 7 icon libraries, color schemes, complete examples, and best practices.
